---
summary: 'Every field in app.json: identity, tables, actions, queries, knobs.'
title: 'app.json'
---

# `app.json`

The manifest. One per app. It is the single source of truth for _what handlers exist, what input each accepts, and what each returns_. The dispatcher reads it on every invocation and validates the caller's `input` against the declared schema before running a handler. Agents discover an app's surface through it via `centraid_describe`.

## Top-level shape

```json
{
  "manifestVersion": 1,
  "id": "hydrate",
  "name": "Hydrate",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "description": "Track 8 cups a day.",
  "kind": "app",
  "tables": [ ... ],
  "actions": [ ... ],
  "queries": [ ... ],
  "knobs": [ ... ]
}
```

| Field             | Type                    | Required | Notes                                                                                                     |
| ----------------- | ----------------------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `manifestVersion` | integer                 | yes      | Must be exactly `1`. The dispatcher rejects any other value (`unsupported_manifest_version`).             |
| `id`              | string                  | yes      | Matches the folder name and URL slug. Lowercase `a–z` / `0–9` / `-`, 1–63 chars, no leading `_`, no dots. |
| `name`            | string                  | yes      | Human-readable. Shown in the desktop sidebar and gallery.                                                 |
| `version`         | string                  | yes      | A non-empty string (semver by convention).                                                                |
| `description`     | string                  | no       | Used by the gallery card and surfaced to agents through `centraid_describe`.                              |
| `kind`            | `"app" \| "automation"` | no       | Defaults to `"app"`. `"automation"` marks a UI-less app that only hosts automations.                      |
| `tables`          | array                   | no       | Documents the schema your handlers expect. The authoritative schema lives in `migrations/`.               |
| `actions`         | array                   | no       | Each entry describes one handler in `actions/<name>.js`. Defaults to `[]`.                                |
| `queries`         | array                   | no       | Each entry describes one handler in `queries/<name>.js`. Defaults to `[]`.                                |
| `knobs`           | array                   | no       | User-facing settings the desktop renders.                                                                 |

Only `manifestVersion`, `id`, `name`, and `version` are required. The manifest schema sets `additionalProperties: true`, so unknown top-level keys are accepted (and ignored) rather than rejected — but don't rely on that to smuggle config; keep extension data in your own files.

## `tables[]`

Documents the schema your handlers expect. **The gateway never creates tables from this** — that is the job of `migrations/`. The array exists for tooling: agents reading the manifest use it to know what shape to ask about.

```json
{
  "name": "hydrate_daily",
  "columns": [
    { "name": "date", "type": "TEXT" },
    { "name": "cups", "type": "INTEGER" }
  ]
}
```

`name` is required on each table; `columns` is optional, and each column needs a `name` (its `type` is optional, free-form text).

## `actions[]` and `queries[]`

**Each entry is a tool definition.** This is the app's _tool catalog_ — the contract by which your UI, an AI agent, the CLI, and any other caller discover what your app can do. The implementation lives in `actions/<name>.js` or `queries/<name>.js`; the entry here is what every caller sees first. See [Queries and actions](/concepts/queries-and-actions) for the framing.

The two arrays share most fields and differ in semantics (read vs write) and which generic tool routes to them — `centraid_read` for queries, `centraid_write` for actions. One field differs: `confirmation` and `writes` are action-only; `reads` is query-only.

```json
{
  "name": "set-cups",
  "description": "Set today's cup count (clamped 0..8).",
  "confirmation": "none",
  "input": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": { "cups": { "type": "number", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 8 } },
    "required": ["cups"],
    "additionalProperties": false
  },
  "output": {
    "type": "object",
    "properties": {
      "date": { "type": "string" },
      "cups": { "type": "number" },
      "goal": { "type": "number" }
    }
  },
  "writes": ["hydrate_daily"]
}
```

| Field          | Type                        | Notes                                                                                                              |
| -------------- | --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `name`         | string                      | Required. Maps to `actions/<name>.js` or `queries/<name>.js`. May not start with `_` (reserved for built-ins).     |
| `description`  | string                      | Surfaced to agents in `centraid_describe`. Write it like a tool description.                                       |
| `confirmation` | `"none" \| "required"`      | **Action only, required.** A chat-side policy hint — see below.                                                    |
| `input`        | JSON Schema (draft 2020-12) | Required. Validated by Ajv before the handler runs. A mismatch returns `INVALID_INPUT` and the handler never runs. |
| `output`       | JSON Schema                 | Documentation only — **not validated at runtime**.                                                                 |
| `writes`       | string[]                    | Action only. Tables the handler is expected to mutate. Documentation for chat permissions — see the note below.    |
| `reads`        | string[]                    | Query only. Tables the handler reads. Documentation; not enforced.                                                 |

A name may appear in both `actions` and `queries` (different tools, different files). Two entries with the same name _within the same array_ are rejected (`duplicate_handler`).

> `writes` and `reads` are **documentation**, not behavior. The change stream does **not** read `writes` to decide what to invalidate — it observes the tables your SQL actually touched via a SQLite session. Keeping `writes` honest helps chat permissions and readers, but it has no effect on which `change` events fire. See [UI and changes](/build/ui-and-changes#the-change-stream).

### About `confirmation`

`confirmation` is **action-only and required**. It is exactly one of:

- `"none"` — fire without prompting (safe, undoable actions).
- `"required"` — the chat/agent surface asks the user to confirm before invoking. Use for destructive or irreversible actions (delete, send, charge).

The dispatcher itself is permissionless — it always runs the handler. `confirmation` is a policy the _chat/agent surface_ honors before invoking autonomously; it is not enforced when a UI button calls the action directly.

## `knobs[]`

User-facing settings. Each renders as a control in the desktop's per-app settings popover.

```json
{
  "key": "appFont",
  "label": "Font",
  "type": "segmented",
  "default": "sans",
  "options": [
    { "value": "sans", "label": "Sans" },
    { "value": "serif", "label": "Serif" },
    { "value": "mono", "label": "Mono" }
  ]
}
```

Each knob requires `key`, `label`, `type`, `default`, and `options` (every option needs `value` + `label`). The `key` is a camel-cased `app*` settings key. There are exactly two `type` values:

| `type`      | Renders as                             | Notes                                        |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `segmented` | A segmented control (one row of pills) | Best for 2–4 discrete options.               |
| `swatch`    | A row of color swatches                | `value` is a hex color; `label` is its name. |

The runtime persists the chosen value, bakes it into `<html data-<key>>` / CSS vars on the served page, and live-updates the iframe via `postMessage`. Knob values arrive in your iframe through the `centraid:settings` message — see [UI and changes](/build/ui-and-changes#receiving-settings).

## Validation, in short

Two passes run when the manifest is loaded:

1. **Ajv** against the manifest's own meta-schema — catches missing required fields and type errors with a JSON path.
2. **Cross-cuts** — `manifestVersion` must equal `1`; no duplicate handler names within an array; no `_`-prefixed handler names.

A malformed `app.json` surfaces as `INVALID_MANIFEST` (or `unsupported_manifest_version`) and the app won't serve until it's fixed.
